title: Mod Runtime Architecture
use_for: designing a reusable runtime foundation for a Unity-hosted game mod
separating loader entry, hooks, probes, facades, modules, UI, diagnostics, and domain mechanics
building deterministic runtime ownership around unstable game state
planning a greenfield mod rewrite around explicit infrastructure boundaries
do_not_use_for: pure gameplay calculation without Unity or mod runtime constraints
ordinary Unity application architecture outside modding constraints
rules: keep loader entry thin and move runtime ownership into explicit hosts and modules
convert raw game callbacks into internal lifecycle events or snapshots
place fragile integration behind hooks, probes, adapters, or facades
make readiness, degraded state, and failure reasons observable
keep domain mechanics independent from Unity objects and raw game APIs where practical
procedure: Define the loader entry boundary and the runtime host it starts.
Define core infrastructure: logging, diagnostics, configuration, main-thread dispatch, events, modules, and shutdown.
Define game integration roles: hooks for notification, probes for discovery, facades for stable access, mutation scopes for writes.
Define lifecycle snapshots for menu, loading, session, vehicle/entity availability, and teardown as needed.
Define feature modules and their dependencies on lifecycle readiness.
Define UI and diagnostics surfaces as observers of published state.
Verify that missing hooks or invalid game state degrade features instead of corrupting runtime state.

primary_when: the task is primarily about designing a Unity game mod's runtime foundation
must_not_own_when: the task is only about one Harmony patch
the task is only about IMGUI layout

solves: how to structure a mod runtime around unstable host lifecycle and private integrations
how to keep feature modules away from raw hooks and probes
how to make degraded state visible and recoverable

core_principles: A mod runtime must treat the host game as unstable external infrastructure.
Hooks notify; probes discover; facades stabilize; modules decide; diagnostics explain.
Feature modules should consume stable lifecycle state rather than raw game callbacks.
Domain mechanics should be portable across runtime surfaces when practical.
constraints: Do not bind reusable guidance to a named game or mod.
Do not let feature modules depend directly on Harmony patch methods.
Do not keep live Unity object references across lifecycle resets without invalidation.
Do not require optional integrations to be present for core diagnostics to run.
decision_rules: If a host event is fragile, wrap it as an internal lifecycle event with diagnostics.
If a host object may disappear, expose a snapshot or facade that handles invalidation.
If a feature mutates game state, require readiness and a mutation boundary.
If a module cannot start, mark it degraded or faulted with a reason.
If domain logic needs game data, provide sampled inputs rather than raw objects.

avoid_patterns: raw game objects as global state
startup order hidden in static constructors
UI as domain source of truth

recipes.name: Design a mod runtime foundation
recipes.when: Use when a mod is being rebuilt or expanded beyond one isolated feature.
steps: Keep the loader entry responsible only for attaching and detaching the runtime.
Register modules with deterministic lifecycle and readiness.
Convert host callbacks into internal events and snapshots.
Route feature work through modules, domain services, pipelines, and mutation boundaries.

pitfalls: Building the runtime around the first hook that happened to work.
Letting game-specific names leak into reusable architecture skills.
Making diagnostics depend on the systems they need to diagnose.
Treating scene or entity references as permanent.
review_checklist: Are hooks, probes, facades, modules, and mechanics separated?
Are lifecycle resets and stale references handled?
Is the guidance free of named-project coupling?

references.title: Runtime Integration
references.interpretation: Use for mod runtime phases, modules, hooks, probes, facades, and diagnostics.
references.why: Unity-hosted mods need explicit runtime integration policy.

references.title: Diagnostics And Readiness
references.interpretation: Use for degraded state, readiness, and failure reporting.
